title: Fix volumes creating for client API

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce*, *sshnaidm* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Not sure if this is the right place to ask, but what's the release cadence typically like for this project?  My IDE doesn't automatically tell me when a new version is out, but this fix would be very helpful for me.  I can work around for now by spinning up podman directly and using command line args, but would also like to have a [rough] idea for when this will be available in a release.
